name: From the Presa de la Fuente del Ciervo by the Ermita de San Isidro — circular by the Picón
name_original: De la Presa de la Fuente del Ciervo por la Ermita de San Isidro — circular por el Picón
region_intro: Hiking around Picón offers a diverse landscape characterized by volcanic formations, tranquil lagoons, and historical sites. The region features rolling terrain with moderate elevation changes, providing accessible routes for various skill levels. Hikers can explore trails that pass by ancient castles, dams, and reservoirs, showcasing both natural beauty and cultural heritage. Best hiking trails around Picón The most popular hiking route is Alarcos Trail, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail explores a volcanic landscape and a serene lagoon. Local hikers also love the Mortara Castle - Miraflores Castle - Tabla de la Hiedra Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through historical castle grounds and open plains, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Picón is defined by volcanic features, historical castles, and reservoir paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Picón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 49 reviews. More than 358 hikers have used komoot to explore Picón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Picón?
A: Picón offers a diverse network of over 45 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from gentle walks to more challenging paths, allowing you to explore the region's unique volcanic formations, tranquil lagoons, and historical sites.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Picón?
A: Hiking in Picón reveals a landscape characterized by distinctive volcanic formations and serene lagoons. You'll also encounter rolling terrain, historical castles, dams, and reservoirs, showcasing a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. The region's environment shares characteristics with areas featuring unique desert ecosystems and volcanic peaks, offering varied vistas.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Picón?
A: Yes, Picón has 13 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. These trails generally feature moderate elevation changes and are accessible for various skill levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198513805/alarcos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, while moderate in length, has relatively flat terrain, making it approachable.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Picón?
A: Yes, Picón offers several circular hiking routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1205916328/from-the-presa-de-la-fuente-del-ciervo-by-the-ermita-de-san-isidro-circular-by-the-picon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Presa de la Fuente del Ciervo by the Ermita de San Isidro — circular by the Picón&lt;/a&gt; trail, which provides a comprehensive experience of the local scenery. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197977248/posadilla-volcano-and-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through volcanic landscapes and past a tranquil lagoon.

Q: What are some notable historical sites or landmarks I can explore on a hike?
A: Picón's trails often lead to fascinating historical sites and landmarks. You can explore ancient castles, such as those featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197409802/mortara-castle-miraflores-castle-tabla-de-la-hiedra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mortara Castle - Miraflores Castle - Tabla de la Hiedra Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the region is home to significant man-made structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which is a prominent feature in the landscape.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies like lagoons or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its tranquil lagoons and reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197977248/posadilla-volcano-and-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posadilla Volcano and Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores a serene lagoon. You can also find trails that pass by the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Vicario Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and opportunities to visit viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3582372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Picón?
A: The hiking routes in Picón are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.6 stars from over 49 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, which includes volcanic features and historical sites, and appreciate the well-maintained paths that offer options for all ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers in Picón?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Picón offers a few more demanding routes. One such trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1205889629/camino-del-sancho-rey-y-embalse-de-el-vicario-circular-desde-las-casas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Sancho Rey y Embalse de El Vicario — circular desde Las Casas&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated as difficult and covers a significant distance, providing a rewarding experience for those looking for a strenuous outing.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Picón?
A: Given the region's characteristics, which can include desert-like environments and volcanic terrain, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Picón. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is often at its best. Summers can be hot, and while winters are generally mild, checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails in Picón?
A: Yes, Picón's trails offer numerous viewpoints and scenic spots. You can enjoy panoramic vistas from locations such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posadilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3582372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which often overlook the reservoirs and the surrounding rolling terrain. Many routes also provide expansive views of the volcanic landscape and historical features.

Q: What kind of wildlife and flora might I encounter on hikes around Picón?
A: While specific details on wildlife and flora for Picón are not extensively documented, regions with similar desert and volcanic characteristics often host resilient plant life adapted to arid conditions, such as various grasses, shrubs, and possibly some unique desert flowers depending on the season. Birdwatching can be rewarding, especially around water bodies like lagoons and reservoirs. Keep an eye out for local fauna that thrive in these environments.
tips: The population was traditionally supplied from the Fuente del Ciervo reservoir, located 5 km from the municipality. Currently Picón is supplied from the Gasset Reservoir.
Really beautiful views and located in a privileged enclave.
